sections.heading: Follow-Up
sections.content: After complete resection without neoadjuvant therapy, history and physical (H&P) and imaging (CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast or MRI) are recommended every 3–6 months for 5 years, then annually. After 10 years, surveillance should be individualized [GIST-3, GIST-F]. For low-risk tumors, less frequent surveillance may be acceptable [GIST-F]. Surveillance aims to detect recurrence early, as treatment with TKIs is effective for recurrent or metastatic disease. Recurrence patterns indicate that most recurrences occur within the first 5 years, but late recurrences are possible, necessitating long-term monitoring. Median time to recurrence after resection of high-risk GIST is about 2 years [49-51]. Recurrence can be locoregional or metastatic, commonly to the liver and peritoneum [MS-2]. Risk of recurrence is influenced by tumor size, mitotic rate, site, and mutation status [6,24,25]. Monitor for cardiac toxicity with imatinib and sunitinib: echocardiography or left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) assessment if symptoms arise [60,67]. Screen for hypothyroidism with sunitinib every 3–6 months via thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) testing [68]. Assess for other TKI-related effects such as skin changes, hypertension, and fatigue during follow-up visits. Evaluate for bone health in patients on long-term TKI therapy, as some may be at risk for osteoporosis.

timeline.title: 0–5 years
timeline.body: History and physical (H&P) and imaging (CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast or MRI) every 3–6 months.

timeline.title: 5–10 years
timeline.body: History and physical (H&P) and imaging (CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast or MRI) annually.

timeline.title: Low-risk tumors
timeline.body: Less frequent surveillance may be acceptable [GIST-F].

late_effects.name: Cardiac toxicity
late_effects.detail: Monitor for cardiac toxicity with imatinib and sunitinib: echocardiography or left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) assessment if symptoms arise [60,67].

late_effects.name: Hypothyroidism
late_effects.detail: Screen for hypothyroidism with sunitinib every 3–6 months via thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) testing [68].

late_effects.name: TKI-related effects
late_effects.detail: Assess for other TKI-related effects such as skin changes, hypertension, and fatigue during follow-up visits.

late_effects.name: Bone health
late_effects.detail: Evaluate for bone health in patients on long-term TKI therapy, as some may be at risk for osteoporosis.
